_about: Per-thesis disconfirmation registry (sidecar). For each ticker: the fastest way the thesis is wrong, the specific + checkable invalidating evidence, and the flip trigger that changes the call. Keyed by ticker under 'entries'. Owner: Claude Code (CC-C). This file is a SIDECAR: it never edits theses.json and never surfaces cards on its own. Seeded entries are status 'DRAFT - operator to confirm' - Claude does not invent invalidation logic as fact; an operator must review and set status to CONFIRMED before an entry counts as a real kill-switch. See docs/disconfirmation_gaps.md for everything still missing or DRAFT.

statuses.DRAFT — operator to confirm: Starter reasoning seeded by Claude Code; reasoned, specific, and checkable, but NOT operator-confirmed fact. Counts as a gap until confirmed.
statuses.CONFIRMED: Operator-reviewed and accepted as the live disconfirmation / kill-switch for this thesis.

NVDA.fastest_way_wrong: AI data-center demand decelerates faster than NVDA can broaden into new markets - hyperscaler capex digestion or a credible compute-efficiency shock breaks the GPU upgrade cycle.
NVDA.invalidating_evidence: Two consecutive quarters of sequential Data Center revenue decline; or 2+ of the top hyperscalers (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META) guiding forward capex down year-over-year on the same earnings cycle; or next-gen (Blackwell/Rubin) gross margin guided materially below the recent ~70%+ on demand - not supply - grounds.
NVDA.flip_trigger: Sequential Data Center revenue turns negative for two straight quarters, OR an aggregate hyperscaler capex guide-down for the forward year - downgrade the secular-compute call to neutral pending re-confirmation.

BMNR.fastest_way_wrong: Held as leveraged / treasury-style ETH exposure (crypto/eth factor tags); the thesis breaks if ETH enters a structural downtrend or the equity de-rates to / below the value of its ETH-per-share, so it stops being an accretive way to own ETH.
BMNR.invalidating_evidence: ETH closing below its key multi-month support on a sustained basis (operator to set the level); OR BMNR trading at or under its ETH net-asset value (market cap <= underlying ETH holdings, mNAV <= 1) for several weeks; OR equity / at-the-market raises that dilute rather than grow ETH-per-share.
BMNR.flip_trigger: Premium-to-NAV gone (price <= ETH-per-share) for a sustained stretch, OR a structural ETH support break - either flips BMNR from accretive leverage to leveraged downside and forces a re-rate of the Generational tag (already flagged CRITICALLY_BELOW the T1 floor).

LEU.fastest_way_wrong: The domestic-enrichment / HALEU thesis breaks if U.S. policy support for onshore enrichment stalls or a competing Western HALEU supplier erodes Centrus's near-monopoly, removing the enrichment-pricing power.
LEU.invalidating_evidence: Non-renewal or cancellation of the DOE HALEU contract; a competing Western HALEU line reaching commercial output; or SWU / enrichment prices rolling over while commercial offtake stays absent.
LEU.flip_trigger: DOE deprioritizes domestic-enrichment funding OR a key HALEU award is cancelled or granted to a competitor - removing the policy-backed monopoly that underpins the Generational tag.

MU.fastest_way_wrong: The memory up-cycle rolls over - DRAM/HBM oversupply or HBM share loss reverses pricing power faster than AI-memory demand can absorb it.
MU.invalidating_evidence: Two consecutive quarters of declining DRAM average selling prices / bit pricing; rising days-of-inventory; or a confirmed HBM3E/HBM4 qualification loss at a major accelerator customer to SK Hynix or Samsung.
MU.flip_trigger: DRAM ASPs decline for two straight quarters OR a public HBM qualification loss at a top customer - flip the cyclical-upturn call to late-cycle pending re-confirmation.
